title: Monorepo + pnpm + TS/ESM + static-site + CI (build/test/lint) skeleton
context: Incidence-Explorers needs an agent-neutral, TypeScript/ESM monorepo skeleton before any data pipeline, dashboard, or content work can begin. The repo must follow Elyos engineering conventions (pnpm workspaces, TS/ESM, MIT code license) and have green CI (build/test/lint) from the start. Static-first delivery is chosen to minimise the threat surface — the product holds no patient or restricted data. This is a pure infrastructure task with low risk and no patient-facing content.
objective: Bootstrap the incidence-explorers monorepo: pnpm workspaces, TypeScript/ESM configuration, a static-site framework scaffold (Astro or Next.js static export), a CI pipeline running build/test/lint, and the package structure for lib/governance, lib/provenance, lib/stats, app/, and content/ as defined in PLAN.md. The skeleton must be agent-neutral (no vendor/agent-specific logic in core packages).
acceptanceCriteria: Monorepo uses pnpm workspaces with TypeScript/ESM configuration per Elyos conventions
Static-site framework (Astro or Next.js static export) is scaffolded and builds successfully
CI pipeline runs build, test, and lint; all three pass on the initial skeleton
Package structure includes stubs for lib/governance, lib/provenance, lib/stats, app/, and content/ as defined in PLAN.md
No vendor/agent-specific logic in any core package (agent-neutral); no secrets, tokens, or PII in committed files
Code licensed MIT; reviewed and merged by the Maintainer
output: A merged PR containing the pnpm/TS/ESM/static-site monorepo skeleton with green CI (build/test/lint), the correct package structure, and no vendor-specific or secrets-containing content — the foundation on which all later code tasks build.
